decisions.reasoning: Since both Incineroars are identical and neither can force a quick KO, Parting Shot is the optimal play to lower the opponent's stats and gain momentum by bringing in a more threatening teammate.

Since both Incineroars are speed-tied and neither can OHKO the other, Knock Off is the optimal play to remove the opponent's item and gain information without taking the recoil damage from Flare Blitz.

Incineroar outspeeds Calyrex-Ice and threatens a 2HKO with Flare Blitz, while Calyrex-Ice only 2HKOs with High Horsepower. Switching to my own Incineroar allows me to Intimidate the opponent and safely pivot or check their physical offense.

Since both Incineroars are at similar health and neither can force a quick KO, Parting Shot is the best play to pivot out, reduce the opponent's offensive stats, and gain momentum by bringing in a more favorable matchup.
Tornadus-Therian is likely to use Hurricane or U-turn; switching to Calyrex-Ice allows me to threaten it with a super-effective STAB Ice move while resisting potential Flying-type attacks.
Glacial Lance is a guaranteed OHKO on Tornadus-Therian. Since I can secure the KO this turn, I should attack immediately to remove the opponent's pivot.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO and deals significant damage to Incineroar. Since I am at 79% HP, I can survive a Flare Blitz and secure the 2HKO on the following turn, maintaining momentum.

Calyrex-Ice is at 18% HP and will be knocked out by any of Rillaboom's moves before it can attack. Switching to Incineroar provides an Intimidate drop to mitigate incoming damage and allows me to safely pivot or threaten the Rillaboom.
Flare Blitz is a guaranteed OHKO on Rillaboom, fulfilling the primary decision priority to secure a KO when available.
